title: Phlegmariurus mollicomus
taxonomy: Phlegmariurus mollicomus is classified within the plant family Lycopodiaceae, which is a group of vascular plants commonly known as club mosses. This classification places Phlegmariurus mollicomus within the division Lycopodiophyta, along with other species of club mosses. Within the family Lycopodiaceae, Phlegmariurus mollicomus is typically placed in the subfamily Lycopodiodeae. The order Lycopodiales is also used to group this species with other related club mosses. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Phlegmariurus mollicomus, also known as Chinese silk fern or soft-leaved climbing fern, is a species of climbing fern native to China and Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The bioactive constituents of Phlegmariurus mollicomus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Phlegmariurus mollicomus is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into formulae to promote respiratory health and relieve symptoms associated with respiratory infections.
